name: Gardening
tagline: Happy plants, one small check-in at a time.

templates.title: Houseplant Care
templates.blurb: A five-minute daily check-in that keeps indoor plants thriving year round.

steps.label: Check soil moisture with a finger
steps.note: Top inch dry = time to water

templates.title: Veggie Patch Starter
templates.blurb: Morning walks and weekend care sessions that turn beds into harvests.

steps.label: Walk the beds and look for changes
Water at the roots, not the leaves

routines.title: Weekend deep care
routines.footer: Skip feeding in a heatwave — stressed plants can't use it.

questions.prompt: How much time can you give it most days?

educationPages.title: Light first
educationPages.body: Light is the one thing you can't fertilize your way around. Match the plant to the window — south-facing is bright, north is gentle — and most 'mystery' problems disappear. A struggling plant usually wants a better spot, not more water.

educationPages.title: The finger test
educationPages.body: Overwatering kills more plants than drought. Push a finger an inch into the soil: dry means water, damp means wait. Pots must drain — roots sitting in water rot quietly.

educationPages.title: Feed in season
educationPages.body: Fertilizer is a boost, not food for the hungry. Feed lightly during spring and summer growth, and stop almost entirely in winter when plants rest. More is not better — it burns roots.

educationPages.title: Catch pests early
educationPages.body: Check leaf undersides once in a while — that's where trouble starts. A weekly wipe and early neem treatment beats fighting an infestation later. Quarantine new plants for a week before they join the others.

products.name: Long-spout watering can
products.what: A can with a narrow, long spout for reaching soil under the leaves.
products.why: Watering at the roots keeps foliage dry, which prevents most fungal problems.
products.order: Use whenever the finger test says the soil is dry.

products.name: Balanced liquid fertilizer
products.what: An all-purpose liquid feed you dilute into your watering can.
products.why: Container plants exhaust their soil — a light feed in the growing season keeps them going.
products.caution: Follow the label dilution — double-strength burns roots. Skip feeding in winter.

products.name: Neem oil spray
products.what: A plant-based oil spray that disrupts common pests like aphids and spider mites.
products.why: Gentle enough for regular use and safe around edibles when used as directed.
products.order: Spray in the evening when you spot pests — never in direct midday sun.
products.caution: Can burn leaves if applied in strong sunlight; test one leaf first.

products.name: Bypass pruning shears
products.what: Sharp scissors-action shears that cut stems cleanly instead of crushing them.
products.why: Clean cuts heal fast; crushed stems invite disease.
products.order: For deadheading, harvesting and shaping on your weekend session.
products.caution: Wipe blades between plants to avoid spreading disease.
